name: Kentucky
tooltip: Kentucky — Cat 3: KRS 391.170. 50-year post-mortem for public figures only. No AI-specific overlay yet.
rightOfPublicity: Yes — KRS § 391.170. Covers commercial use of name or likeness.
postMortem: Yes — 50 years after death, **but only for "public figures."** KRS § 391.170(2). This is unusual — most state post-mortem statutes apply to anyone with commercial value at death, regardless of "public figure" status.
remedy: Civil suit under § 391.170 for actual damages plus profits and equitable relief.
whatsInteresting: Kentucky's "public figures only" qualifier on the post-mortem right is the catch. If you're a public figure (musicians, actors, athletes, well-known commercial personalities), you have 50 years of post-mortem protection. If you're not — say, a successful local businessperson whose face is used commercially — the post-mortem right may not apply at all. The statute doesn't define "public figure" with a clean test, so this gets litigated case by case.
